description: Scanpy preprocessing of an h5 single cell RNASeq dataset
author: Tom Sherman, Johns Hopkins University, modified by Anthony Castanza, UCSD, wrapped as a module by Ted Liefeld, UCSD School of Medicine.
plan:
## ScanpyUtilities GenePattern Module — Implementation Plan

### 1. Overview
ScanpyUtilities is a GenePattern module that Scanpy preprocessing of an h5 single cell RNASeq dataset

### 2. Parameters
The module exposes 23 parameters:
- Required: data.file, output.basename, cell.type.marker.id.type, compute.umap, compute.tsne, normalize, annotate, clustering.resolution
- Optional: mtGenes, genome, cells.min.counts, cells.max.counts, cells.min.genes, cells.max.genes, cells.max.mt.pct, genes.min.counts, genes.max.counts, genes.min.cells, genes.max.cells, cell.type.marker.file, gene.annotation.database, n.high.variance.genes, compute.clustering

### 3. Command Line
```
bash /build/run_module.sh --python.executable\=python --src.path\=/build --data.file\=<data.file> <mtGenes> --output.basename\=<output.basename> <genome> --annotate\=<annotate> <cells.min.counts> <cells.max.counts> <cells.min.genes> <cells.max.genes> <cells.max.mt.pct> <genes.min.counts> <genes.max.counts> <genes.min.cells> <genes.max.cells> <cell.type.marker.file> <gene.annotation.database> <cell.type.marker.id.type> --normalize\=<normalize> <n.high.variance.genes> --compute.umap\=<compute.umap> --compute.tsne\=<compute.tsne> --compute.clustering\=<compute.clustering> --clustering.resolution\=<clustering.resolution>
```

### 4. Docker Image
Docker image: genepattern/scanpyutilities\:0.101

### 5. Validation Strategy
- Validate all required parameters are provided
- Validate file formats match expected extensions
- Check parameter value ranges where applicable

### 6. Implementation Roadmap
Phase 1: Wrapper script implementation
Phase 2: Docker container setup
Phase 3: Parameter validation and error handling
Phase 4: Testing and documentation

parameters.name: data.file
parameters.description: A file containing single-cell data. h5ad, loom and mtx file formats are accepted. A 10x formatted hdf5 file may also be provided along with the 'genome' argument. The file will be internally converted to an h5ad file before proceeding.

parameters.name: mtGenes
parameters.description: A file containing a list of the gene ids for mitochondrial genes. Optional, used for filtering by mitochondrial abundance.

parameters.name: output.basename
parameters.description: Base filename for the output file

parameters.name: genome
parameters.description: When converting a 10x formated HDF5 file, the module will internally call read_10x_h5 from the scanpy package. This function expects a genome argument which specifies the name of the data set in the HDF5 file. e.g. 'GRCh38'

parameters.name: cells.min.counts
parameters.description: Filter out cells with fewer total counts than min counts

parameters.name: cells.max.counts
parameters.description: Filter out cells with more total counts than max counts

parameters.name: cells.min.genes
parameters.description: Filter out cells with fewer than min genes expressed

parameters.name: cells.max.genes
parameters.description: Filter out cells with more than max genes expressed

parameters.name: cells.max.mt.pct
parameters.description: Filter out cells with more than max mitochondrial percentage

parameters.name: genes.min.counts
parameters.description: Filter out genes with fewer total counts than min counts

parameters.name: genes.max.counts
parameters.description: Filter out genes with more total counts than max counts

parameters.name: genes.min.cells
parameters.description: Filter out genes expressed in fewer than min cells

parameters.name: genes.max.cells
parameters.description: Filter out genes expressed in more than max cells

parameters.name: cell.type.marker.file
parameters.description: A text file describing the marker genes for each cell type. This should be in the format accepted by the R package garnett as defined in https\://cole-trapnell-lab.github.io/garnett/docs/. Alternatively, a pre-trained garnett classifier in .RDS format can be supplied.

parameters.name: cell.type.marker.id.type
parameters.description: Choose whether the genes in the cell type marker file are given as Gene Symbols or Ensembl Gene IDs

parameters.name: gene.annotation.database
parameters.description: This can be either "org.Hs.eg.db" (https\://bioconductor.org/packages/release/data/annotation/html/org.Hs.eg.db.html) or "org.Mm.eg.db" (https\://bioconductor.org/packages/release/data/annotation/html/org.Mm.eg.db.html). This annotation database is used to convert the gene names we've provided in the marker file to the gene ids we've used as the index in our data. If this is left blank, an exact match between the marker file and the gene indices is expected.

parameters.name: compute.umap
parameters.description: Whether to compute UMAP True/False.

parameters.name: compute.tsne
parameters.description: Whether to compute TSNE True/False.

parameters.name: n.high.variance.genes
parameters.description: Subset to the top N highly variable genes

parameters.name: normalize
parameters.description: Whether to perform normalization True/False. This step is performed after filtering if filtering is on

parameters.name: annotate
parameters.description: Whether to perform annotation True/False.

parameters.name: clustering.resolution
parameters.description: Resolution to use if running leiden or louvain clustering on the dataset.

parameters.name: compute.clustering
parameters.description: Whether to and type of clustering to perform (leiden, louvain, none) .
